description: Explicit branch authority for the two-branch model. master = production/site/code/architecture/governance authority; main = repository default branch hosting Issue Forms, PR templates and manually dispatched control workflows. The branches are never merged wholesale in either direction.

owns: AI Ops validators used by PRs targeting master

sharedExisting.description: Files that already exist on main and are intentionally left byte-identical by AI Ops V1; not owned by either new PR.

rules: default-branch Issue Form / PR template / control-workflow PRs target main
main and master must never be merged wholesale in either direction
main and master must never be rebased onto each other
cross-authority modifications require separate task contracts
deployment may use an explicitly owner-approved master SHA only
no deploy action may be inferred from a merge
a task contract must not request a wholesale main<->master merge

validation.masterPrForbidsMainOnly: a PR whose baseBranch is master must not change any main-owned path (Issue Forms, PR template, control workflows, default-branch authority doc)
validation.mainPrForbidsMasterOnly: a PR whose baseBranch is main must not change any master-owned prefix or path (application code, owner-ops, scripts, docs, tests, advisory gate, CODEOWNERS, package.json)
validation.wholesaleMergeForbidden: a contract that lists the other authority's whole tree, or declares a main<->master merge, fails branch-authority validation
